methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Jimmy Energy is a Series A nuclear energy company founded by Antoine Guyot and Mathilde Grivet, having raised €80M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 11 peers across the nuclear energy sector: 3 public companies (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private firms including Fuse Energy, Oklo, TerraPower, X-Energy, and Kairos Power.

Standard peer-median methodology is not applicable here. The nuclear energy sector lacks sufficient same-stage, adjacent-stage, and valuation-tier-matched peers—only 2 datapoints meet narrow filters when a minimum of 3 is required for statistical reliability. An all-sector median would introduce material bias given the heterogeneity of business models, technology maturity, and capital intensity within the broader nuclear cohort. The private peer set spans early venture through late-stage development, and public comparables trade at scales not yet relevant to Series A positioning.

This constraint leaves valuation highly dependent on qualitative investment thesis validation: technology differentiation, pathway to capital deployment, and team execution history relative to the identified peer universe. Quantitative triangulation cannot be responsibly completed with available data. Prospective analysis would benefit from (1) establishing clear technology-bucket taxonomy within nuclear (advanced reactors, fuel, infrastructure), (2) identifying 3+ same-stage peers within that narrow tier, or (3) building bottoms-up models anchored to specific project timelines and unit economics.
